mappings.rationaleAI: The evolution of teeth from dermal denticles to differentiated mammalian dentition covers cuticular skeletal elements (bone, enamel), the vertebrate skeleton's dental component, and properties of bone development as teeth are modified skeletal structures.

The evolution of lungs covers the diaphragm as a muscular structure in mammals and ventilation mechanics involving skeletal and smooth muscle, relevant to vertebrate muscle systems and their evolution.

Cephalopods have jet propulsion (muscular system), chromatophores (integumentary color change), and hydrostatic skeletons, illustrating invertebrate integuments, invertebrate skeleton organization, and invertebrate muscle systems.
